1. Sports Club  Introduction, Objectives, Vision, Motto and Games
Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_01 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab1 | ~380 tokens
Context: Describes the MSAJCE Sports Club  its purpose, objectives, vision, motto, and the list of indoor and outdoor games played,
useful for students wanting to know sports facilities and activities.
MSAJCE Sports Club
This club maintains records of sports and games events attended by students within the college, within the university and
outside at the region/state/national level and their achievements/awards. It submits annual reports on sports/events and budget
allocations and spending during the year. It conducts sports events that students need and rewards them with trophies and
medals. All kinds of sports events take place in this club.
1. To build up sportsmanship.
2. To foster intimate friendships.
3. To reduce stress and improve health.
4. To improve skills and enhance employability.
5. To support in developing positive attitude, self-confidence, courage and patience.
6. To get opportunities to see the community closely and thus get an experience of human nature in relation to his/her
environment.
7. To avail the opportunity to meet people from different walks of life.
Vision:
To encourage and motivate students to involve in various sports activities at both inter and intra college level.
Motto: "Teamwork Makes The Dream Work"
Games Conducted:
Outdoor Games:

Cricket, Football, Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby, Badminton, Kho Kho, Throughball
Indoor Games:
Carrom, Table Tennis, Chess
Sports Club  Games Offered
Type Games
Outdoor Cricket, Football, Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby, Badminton, Kho Kho, Throughball
Indoor Carrom, Table Tennis, Chess

2. Fine Arts Club (ENVISTA CLUB  ARTFUL AESTHETICS)  Introduction, Objectives, Vision,
Motto and Student Leaders
Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_02 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab2 | ~430 tokens
Context: Describes the MSAJCE Fine Arts Club under the ENVISTA CLUB umbrella, including its 8 objectives, vision, motto, staff
coordinator, and current student president and vice president.
MSAJCE Fine Arts Club  ARTFUL AESTHETICS (Part of ENVISTA CLUB)
Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering has successfully inaugurated the ENVISTA CLUB. In total, 10 clubs are going to
take part actively in this umbrella.
1. The main objective of the fine arts club is to encourage the budding talent of the students.
2. Impart skill-based training and multi-disciplinary research activities with industry and reputed institute collaborations. Provide
opportunities and encouragement to express oneself freely.
3. Educational objectives of this club are to encourage imagination, creative ability and introduce an artistic environment.
4. Inspiring students to make maximum contributions as creative artists and citizens.
5. Organize various competitions, functions, and events in the college.
6. Make the student confident and competitive in the global environment.
7. To improve the imagination skill in entertainment.
8. To give students an artistic outlet and to help each enrich interest for art and personal experience through art.
Staff Coordinator: S. Viswajaa  Assistant Professor, IT Department
Motto:
The motto of the Fine Arts Club is to inspire and enable students to actively participate and develop their talent and skills like
Dance, Art, Singing, Music, Acting etc. Through this club students can develop their skills and talents as well as feel relaxation
and enjoy their college life. The club plans to conduct several events for extra-curricular activities. Students are free to
participate in this club and expose their talents. The club also makes students participate in external college fests to showcase

their talents.
Vision:
To encourage students to express their thoughts, feelings and creativity through various visual art forms from two dimensions to
three dimensions. To tap the inherent talents and potentials of the student community at all levels.
Student President: Kishore P  IV Year Mechanical
Student Vice President: Laksha  III Year CSE

3. Science Club  Introduction, Objectives and Activities
Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_03 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab3 | ~430 tokens
Context: Describes the MSAJCE Science Club  its purpose of inviting scientists, three sections (Physics, Chemistry, General), guiding
principles, and planned activities including experiments and workshops.
MSAJCE Science Club
The Science Club was started with an aim to invite eminent scientists who would share their experience with students and
motivate them to gain scientific knowledge. Activities are designed primarily to introduce students to undertake interesting and
exciting science and technology projects. The Science Club consists of a group of students who want to explore the many
branches of engineering, mathematics, and science. The club is open to all students.
The purpose of the Science Club is to promote interest, understanding, and knowledge of the scientific world amongst college
students and the local community. Science Club is one of the largest clubs in the college. The club is divided into three sections:
Physics, Chemistry, and General.
The club aims to arouse engineering students' interest in learning science, promoting academic achievement in Science
subjects. It also helps students to learn more about science outside the classroom and teaches how to apply scientific
knowledge in daily life through activities. The club also guides students to develop an independent, logical and creative mind.
Activities include experiments in: making electronic components, making invisible ink, electroplating, biodiesels, fire writing, and
making venation of leaves. Information about the science club and day-to-day updates are displayed on the notice board.
Objectives of Science Club:
The five Guiding Principles govern how activities have been developed. Investigator activity offers students the opportunity to:
1. Solve a relevant, science-based problem set within a scenario.
2. Work in pairs or small groups or independently.
3. Take part in practical, hands-on science activities.
4. Think and talk about science during the activity and when sharing results.
5. Share results using a variety of media.
Things to Do in Science Club:

(cid:127) Conduct Seminars, Workshops, Exhibitions and Quiz competitions to inculcate knowledge in the field of science.
(cid:127) Make students acquire knowledge by giving lectures once a month.
(cid:127) Promote the use of IT in learning science.
(cid:127) Encourage student participation in activities organized by the Science Club.
(cid:127) Help students promote discussion on recent innovations in the field of science once a month.
(cid:127) Decorate the walls of the classroom, library and laboratory with scientific pictures and charts prepared by students themselves.

4. Tamil Mandram  Introduction, Objectives and Events
Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_04 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab4 | ~410 tokens
Context: Describes the MSAJCE Tamil Mandram  a club dedicated to promoting Tamil language, culture and ancient heritage among
students, with its objectives and list of cultural events conducted.
MSAJCE Tamil Mandram (Tamil Language and Culture Club)
The Tamil Mandram was formed to bring about a wonderful transformation in the youth to keep the Tamil language alive and
thriving. The motto of the Mandram is:
"Thithikkum then tamil mozhi endrum vaazha vendum! Engum vella vendum!"
(Translation: "The sweet honey Tamil language must live forever! Must win everywhere!")
The Mandram works to revive and reconstruct the ancient Tamil culture and the identities left behind by ancestors. The vision is
for the youth to take pride in their language and move from just speaking Tamil to actively bringing glory to Tamil through action.
"Vaazha Tamil! Velga Tamil!"
(Translation: "Long live Tamil! Victory to Tamil!")
Objectives of Tamil Mandram:
1. To develop Tamil enthusiasm and knowledge among students  this is the primary objective of the Tamil Mandram.
2. To spread ancient Tamil culture and heritage among today's youth.
3. To develop technical knowledge in Tamil.
4. To continuously conduct Tamil events and social activities suited to the times.
Events Conducted by Tamil Mandram:
1. Irumugam Oru Agam (Two faces, one heart  elocution/drama)
2. Pessum Padam (Speaking Picture  storytelling)
3. Aadu Puli (Tiger and Goat  traditional game)
4. Kaivanna Kaviyam (Colourful Poetry  art and poetry)
5. Tent Kottai (Tent Camp  cultural event)
6. Vidugadhai Vilaiyattu (Riddle Games)

7. Pirasangatin Thalaiwan (King of Speeches  debate/oratory competition)
8. Tamilkkorvai (Tamil Chain  literary event)
Tamil Mandram Events

5. Coding Club  About, Aim and Practice
Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_05 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab5 | ~360 tokens
Context: Describes the MSAJCE Coding Club  its aims to promote coding contests, workshops on web development, ML and IoT,
participation in ACM-ICPC and Google events, and weekly club activities.
MSAJCE Coding Club
About:
Programming abilities are crucial tools that can be applied to and integrated into a variety of disciplines and domains in today's
climate of rapid change.
Aim:
1. More coding contests should be held to reach all students on campus.
2. Conduct workshops on computer science topics like web development, application development, machine learning, IoT, etc.
3. Participate in open source initiatives, create websites and applications, and offer technical assistance to clubs and groups on
campus.
4. Represent the college at intercollegiate competitions.
5. Take part in competitions like ACM-ICPC, Google Code Jam, Google Summer of Code, and other online coding events.
Practice:

1. The club holds a college-wide coding competition to find new members who are enthusiastic about coding. The competition
emphasizes reasoning, analysis, and problem-solving abilities in addition to coding.
2. The club gets together once a week to talk and work on initiatives. To accomplish a shared goal, all members work together.
Workshops and technical discussions on various competitions and computer science topics are conducted through the club.
3. Lecture sessions are also organized for the benefit of members, where some computer science principles are taught.
4. The group also hosts seminars and workshops by seniors and former members on how to prepare for internships and job
placement.

6. Robotic Club  Introduction, Aim, Objectives and Activities
Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_06 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab6 | ~290 tokens
Context: Describes the MSAJCE Robotic Club  its purpose of bringing together students interested in robotics, its aim to cultivate
technological thinking, and its activities including training sessions and workshops.
MSAJCE Robotic Club
Students that are interested in learning about and using robots can come together to form a robotics club. Teams of students
must design and construct a robot that can compete against opponents in a number of distinct challenges in order to win the
robotics tournament.
Aim:
The Robotics Club aims to cultivate the habit of 'thinking technologically' inside each of us.
1. To encourage young people to become the robotics industry's driving force.
2. It is an opportunity to creatively use information and observe how systems interact with the environment and one another.
3. To give students the professional engineering skills that will benefit them in the workplace.
Club Activities Include:
1. Robotics Education Initiatives
2. Brief Training Sessions
3. Robotics Basics Workshop

7. Energy and Eco Club  Energy Club and Eco Club
Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_07 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab7 | ~300 tokens
Context: Describes the two components of the MSAJCE Energy and Eco Club  the Energy Club focused on sustainable energy
awareness and conservation, and the Eco Club focused on environmental responsibility.
MSAJCE Energy and Eco Club
Energy Club:
The goal of the Energy Club is to raise and spread knowledge about responsible and sustainable energy use. Given the limited
quantity of fossil fuels available, resource management and conservation are urgently needed. Students are introduced to the
concept of energy conservation through the Energy Club, as well as the necessity for it and what part they can play in
guaranteeing conservation. In order to combat the ongoing energy issue, it helps the students to conserve energy. These
actions would support not only the dissemination of information about energy efficiency but also its application in homes and
institutions.
Eco Club:
The Eco Club holds that nature imparts life lessons in its own steady ways and empowers our young students to share their
responsibility for upholding environmental health and taking action to prevent environmental degradation both individually and
collectively. The club encourages students to take both individual and collective action to protect the environment.

8. Photography Club  Introduction and Activities
Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_08 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab8 | ~360 tokens
Context: Describes the MSAJCE Photography Club  its encouragement of photography as a hobby, skills development in composition
and creative expression, photo editing using software, and coverage of school events.
MSAJCE Photography Club
The members of the photography club are encouraged to take images as they grow to appreciate the hobby more. It gives
workers and students a venue for exploring and appreciating their surroundings through photography, short filmmaking, and
screenwriting.

There is much more to photography than just using a camera to take photographs. By comprehending and learning about what
constitutes a successful photograph  from composition to creative expression  students in this club have the chance to
develop their skills. Additionally, club members will learn how to edit and improve photographs using cutting-edge software for
both personal use and publication.
While taking pictures of sporting events and activities relevant to the school, students are encouraged to hone their talents.
Exploring this fascinating aspect of the visual arts is welcomed and encouraged for people of all expertise levels.
Key Skills Developed:
1. Photography composition and creative expression
2. Photo editing using advanced software
3. Short filmmaking and screenwriting
4. Event photography (sports events, college activities)
5. Visual arts exploration
The club is open to all expertise levels  beginners and advanced photographers are both welcome.
